text.title: rust-testing-ci-toolchain Failure Modes
text.summary: Failure modes observed when applying the rust-testing-ci-toolchain methodology. Each pairs symptom + root-cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: CI keeps cargo test --workspace as primary and adds nextest only as a parallel job, doubling CI minutes.
antipattern.root-cause: Author migrated without removing the legacy step.
antipattern.fix: Delete the cargo test step; keep only `cargo nextest run --workspace --profile ci`.

antipattern.symptom: taiki-e/install-action uses tool: nextest without a pin; a release changes flag names and CI breaks.
antipattern.fix: Pin via tool: nextest@0.9.78; add Renovate config entry for the install-action.

antipattern.symptom: Miri job runs in the same needs: chain; one flaky UB-detection failure blocks unrelated PRs.
antipattern.root-cause: Author wired miri into the critical path.
antipattern.fix: Move miri to a separate job gated by a PR label so it never blocks orthogonal PRs.

antipattern.symptom: CI fails the build when LCOV diff drops below 80%, leading to test inflation.
antipattern.root-cause: Author treated coverage as binary pass/fail.
antipattern.fix: Remove the percentage gate; keep upload-only, surface diff coverage in PR comments.
